mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real retail sales growth and acceleration in CPI inflation creates a stagflationary environment where corporate margins compress and monetary policy remains restrictive, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields no significant alpha vs baseline (t-stat < 1.5) over 2020-2023 sample.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization and economic reopening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside post-shock recovery regimes or during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth (Spearman rho < 0.05) over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.6% signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Market absorbs high inflation prints without negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicate a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined labor strength signal fails to predict positive returns (accuracy < 50%) during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, rising housing starts reflect improved consumer confidence and credit availability, acting as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts lose predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during easing cycles or show negative correlation with equity returns.
